name: Huygen Hybrid SEO + HN Latest Auto Publisher v8.1 Google Images + 404 Safe

nodes.name: Fetch Hacker News Signals
nodes.notes: Retry policy: retries up to 4 times with 15s between attempts.

parameters.jsCode:
const decision = $('Decide Content Mode').first().json;
const siteUrl = decision.siteUrl;

const topics = [
  {cluster:'AI Automation', topic:'AI automation for small business lead follow-up', keyword:'AI automation for small business', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'AI Automation', topic:'What to automate first in a service business', keyword:'what to automate first in a small business', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'AI Automation', topic:'AI automation workflow checklist for local businesses', keyword:'AI automation workflow checklist', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'AI Automation', topic:'AI automation mistakes that break customer experience', keyword:'AI automation mistakes', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'AI Automation', topic:'AI automation implementation roadmap for founders', keyword:'AI automation implementation roadmap', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'AI Voice Agents', topic:'AI voice agent missed call recovery workflow', keyword:'AI voice agent missed call recovery', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-voice-agents'},
  {cluster:'AI Voice Agents', topic:'AI receptionist versus human receptionist for appointment booking', keyword:'AI receptionist vs human receptionist', intent:'comparison', service:'/services/ai-voice-agents'},
  {cluster:'AI Voice Agents', topic:'AI voice agent appointment booking script for service businesses', keyword:'AI voice agent appointment booking script', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-voice-agents'},
  {cluster:'AI Voice Agents', topic:'How AI voice agents qualify inbound leads', keyword:'AI voice agent lead qualification', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-voice-agents'},
  {cluster:'AI Voice Agents', topic:'AI voice agents for home service companies', keyword:'AI voice agents for home services', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-voice-agents'},
  {cluster:'WhatsApp Automation', topic:'WhatsApp lead qualification flow for clinics and service businesses', keyword:'WhatsApp lead qualification flow', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/whatsapp-automation'},
  {cluster:'WhatsApp Automation', topic:'WhatsApp automation for missed call follow-up', keyword:'WhatsApp automation missed call follow up', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/whatsapp-automation'},
  {cluster:'WhatsApp Automation', topic:'WhatsApp CRM automation for local business sales teams', keyword:'WhatsApp CRM automation', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/whatsapp-automation'},
  {cluster:'WhatsApp Automation', topic:'WhatsApp automation message templates for lead conversion', keyword:'WhatsApp automation templates', intent:'informational', service:'/services/whatsapp-automation'},
  {cluster:'GoHighLevel Automation', topic:'GoHighLevel automation architecture for local businesses', keyword:'GoHighLevel automation architecture', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/gohighlevel-automation'},
  {cluster:'GoHighLevel Automation', topic:'GoHighLevel pipeline design for appointment-based businesses', keyword:'GoHighLevel pipeline design', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/gohighlevel-automation'},
  {cluster:'GoHighLevel Automation', topic:'GoHighLevel missed call text back workflow', keyword:'GoHighLevel missed call text back', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/gohighlevel-automation'},
  {cluster:'CRM Workflows', topic:'CRM pipeline stages for local service businesses', keyword:'CRM pipeline stages for service business', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'CRM Workflows', topic:'Lead scoring workflow for service businesses using AI', keyword:'AI lead scoring workflow', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'CRM Workflows', topic:'How to design CRM follow-up sequences that convert', keyword:'CRM follow up sequences', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Cinematic Websites', topic:'Cinematic website conversion framework for premium brands', keyword:'cinematic website conversion framework', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/cinematic-websites'},
  {cluster:'Cinematic Websites', topic:'Website redesign checklist for service businesses', keyword:'website redesign checklist for service business', intent:'informational', service:'/services/cinematic-websites'},
  {cluster:'Cinematic Websites', topic:'How motion design affects website conversion without hurting speed', keyword:'motion design website conversion', intent:'informational', service:'/services/cinematic-websites'},
  {cluster:'Cinematic Websites', topic:'Cinematic websites versus traditional business websites', keyword:'cinematic website vs traditional website', intent:'comparison', service:'/services/cinematic-websites'},
  {cluster:'SEO and AI Search', topic:'SEO for AI search on business websites', keyword:'SEO for AI search', intent:'informational', service:'/services/cinematic-websites'},
  {cluster:'SEO and AI Search', topic:'How service businesses should structure pages for AI search engines', keyword:'AI search optimization for service business', intent:'informational', service:'/services/cinematic-websites'},
  {cluster:'Creative AI', topic:'Creative AI workflow for social content production', keyword:'creative AI workflow', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Creative AI', topic:'AI tools for creative production teams without losing brand quality', keyword:'AI tools for creative production', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Automation Strategy', topic:'Automation agency tech stack for small business systems', keyword:'automation agency tech stack', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Automation Strategy', topic:'How to calculate ROI before building an automation workflow', keyword:'automation ROI calculation', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Automation Strategy', topic:'How to document business processes before automating them', keyword:'document business processes before automation', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Industry Use Cases', topic:'AI automation for real estate lead follow-up', keyword:'AI automation for real estate leads', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Industry Use Cases', topic:'AI voice agents for dental clinics', keyword:'AI voice agents for dental clinics', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-voice-agents'},
  {cluster:'Industry Use Cases', topic:'WhatsApp automation for gyms and fitness studios', keyword:'WhatsApp automation for gyms', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/whatsapp-automation'},
  {cluster:'Industry Use Cases', topic:'AI automation for law firm intake workflows', keyword:'AI automation for law firms', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Industry Use Cases', topic:'AI automation for med spa lead conversion', keyword:'AI automation for med spas', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Sales Automation', topic:'Speed to lead automation for local service companies', keyword:'speed to lead automation', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Sales Automation', topic:'How to build an automated sales follow-up system', keyword:'automated sales follow up system', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Sales Automation', topic:'How AI agents can reduce no-shows for appointments', keyword:'AI agents reduce no shows', intent:'commercial', service:'/services/ai-voice-agents'},
  {cluster:'Technical Explainers', topic:'How AI agents connect to CRMs, calendars, and messaging tools', keyword:'AI agent CRM calendar integration', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Technical Explainers', topic:'Webhook automation explained for business owners', keyword:'webhook automation explained', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'},
  {cluster:'Technical Explainers', topic:'How to design safe handoff rules for AI agents', keyword:'AI agent human handoff rules', intent:'informational', service:'/services/ai-automation'}
];

function slugify(s) {
  return String(s).toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '').slice(0, 80);
}

const topicalLenses = [
  'agentic AI workflows moving from demos into production operations',
  'AI search and answer-engine visibility for service businesses',
  'CRM-connected AI agents that qualify, route, and follow up with leads',
  'voice agents and WhatsApp automation as front-office infrastructure',
  'human handoff, safety rules, and observability for business automations',
  'creative AI systems for websites, content operations, and premium digital experiences',
  'workflow automation that connects forms, calendars, CRMs, messaging, and reporting',
  'practical AI implementation choices founders should make before buying more software'
];

const now = new Date();
const day = Math.floor(now.getTime() / 86400000);
// one evergreen SEO post/day. Move through the cluster list deterministically.
const index = day % topics.length;
const selected = topics[index];
const topicalLens = topicalLenses[day % topicalLenses.length];
const baseSlug = slugify(selected.topic);
const minWords = $env.MIN_WORD_COUNT || '800';

const articlePrompt = `You are the senior SEO strategist and technical content architect for Huygen Studios, an AI automation and digital experience studio at ${siteUrl}.

Create a production-ready evergreen SEO article for this topic:
Topic: ${selected.topic}
Primary keyword: ${selected.keyword}
Cluster: ${selected.cluster}
Search intent: ${selected.intent}
Relevant service page: ${selected.service}
Topical AI lens to weave in naturally: ${topicalLens}

SEO STRATEGY:
- Satisfy the real search intent first. Do not write a generic template article.
- Use the primary keyword naturally in the title, intro, at least one heading where appropriate, meta fields, and body.
- Add semantically related terms, but avoid keyword stuffing.
- Write for founders, operators, local service businesses, creative teams, and teams considering AI automation.
- Make the article feel current by discussing modern AI agents, AI search, voice agents, WhatsApp automation, CRM workflows, no-code/low-code automation, handoff rules, measurement, and implementation risk where relevant.
- Do not claim breaking news, current market data, statistics, or named product changes unless they are general knowledge inside the prompt. This SEO mode has no external news source.
- Prefer specific implementation thinking over broad inspirational writing.

CONTENT QUALITY RULES:
STRICT WORD COUNT SYSTEM INSTRUCTION:\n- The articleHtml body MUST contain at least ${minWords} words after all HTML tags are removed.\n- Aim for 1000 to 1450 words so the article cannot accidentally fall below the hard floor.\n- Before returning JSON, mentally count the article words. If it is below ${minWords} words, add useful H2/H3 sections, examples, implementation notes, FAQs, or checklists until it is above the hard floor.\n- Never return a short article. Never return fewer words than requested.\n- Quality matters, but length compliance is mandatory.
- Output JSON only. No markdown fences.
- Create original, practical analysis. Do not invent statistics, fake clients, fake results, awards, or fake case studies.
- Do not mention AdSense.
- Use exactly one H1 in articleHtml.
- Use H2/H3 sections, but choose the structure based on the topic instead of forcing the same outline every time.
- Include actionable implementation steps, decision criteria, common mistakes, and practical business implications.
- Include a short FAQ section only if it improves search usefulness for this topic.
- Use short paragraphs and concrete language. Avoid filler, hype, and vague phrases like "in today's digital landscape".
- Naturally include internal links to ${selected.service}, /services/ai-automation, /services/ai-voice-agents, /services/whatsapp-automation, /services/cinematic-websites, /services/gohighlevel-automation, /blog, and /contact where relevant.
- The article should sound premium, technical, commercial, and useful — like a studio that actually implements automations, not like a generic SEO blog.
- Return articleHtml as safe semantic HTML using h1, h2, h3, p, ul, ol, li, strong, em, a. Do not include script/style/iframe.

Return exactly this JSON shape:
{
  "title": "",
  "slug": "${baseSlug}",
  "metaTitle": "",
  "metaDescription": "",
  "excerpt": "",
  "cluster": "${selected.cluster}",
  "primaryKeyword": "${selected.keyword}",
  "sourceMode": "seo",
  "sourceUrls": [],
  "articleHtml": ""
}`;

return [{ json: { siteUrl, sourceMode:'seo', selected, topicalLens, baseSlug, articlePrompt, scheduledAt: now.toISOString() } }];

nodes.name: Gemini Generate Article
nodes.notes:
High-availability Gemini call. Defaults to gemini-3.1-flash-lite to avoid gemini-3.5-flash high-demand spikes. Retries up to 4 times.
Retry policy: retries up to 4 times with 15s between attempts.

nodes.name: Gemini Quality Gate
nodes.notes:
High-availability Gemini quality gate. Uses GEMINI_QUALITY_MODEL or GEMINI_MODEL; default gemini-3.1-flash-lite. Retries transient demand errors.
Retry policy: retries up to 4 times with 15s between attempts.

nodes.name: Prepare Marble Payload
nodes.notes: Builds an image-safe draft Marble payload. The v8.1 Google image stage later replaces or adds coverImage only after the same-origin proxy has been verified.

nodes.name: Upsert to Marble
nodes.notes: Idempotent Marble write. Uses POST for new slugs and PATCH for existing slugs. Returns all HTTP statuses so the workflow can recover from 409 collisions, 404 races, 429s, and 5xx errors.

nodes.name: Revalidate Website
nodes.notes: Optional cache revalidation. The prepared URL includes slug, path, public URL, paths JSON, and optional secret. Failure does not falsely mark success; the public URL verification node is the source of truth.

parameters.jsCode:
const result = $input.first().json;
return [{
  json: {
    status: 'published_verified',
    sourceMode: result.sourceMode,
    title: result.title,
    slug: result.slug,
    publicUrl: result.publicUrl,
    sourceUrls: result.sourceUrls || [],
    coverImage: result.coverImage || null,
    coverImageSource: result.coverImageSource || 'omitted-safe-no-valid-url',
    imageSearchQuery: result.imageSearchQuery || '',
    imageDirectUrl: result.imageDirectUrl || '',
    imageContextUrl: result.imageContextUrl || '',
    imageSourceTitle: result.imageSourceTitle || '',
    imageSourceHost: result.imageSourceHost || '',
    imageProxyVerified: result.imageProxyVerified === true,
    imageProxyStatusCode: result.imageProxyStatusCode || null,
    googleImageSearchStatusCode: result.googleImageSearchStatusCode || null,
    googleImageEligibleCount: result.googleImageEligibleCount || 0,
    imageCreditAppended: result.imageCreditAppended === true,
    marbleWriteMethod: result.marbleWriteMethod,
    marbleWriteStatusCode: result.marbleWriteStatusCode,
    marblePost: result.marblePost,
    publicStatusCode: result.publicStatusCode,
    revalidationAttempted: result.revalidationAttempted,
    revalidationStatusCode: result.revalidationStatusCode || null,
    quality: result.quality,
    publishedAt: new Date().toISOString()
  }
}];

nodes.name: Published Output
nodes.notes: Final success is emitted only after Marble returns the post as published and the public /blog/[slug] URL responds successfully without known error-page markers.

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $input.first().json;
return [{
  json: {
    status: item.status || 'skipped',
    reason: item.blockingReason || item.imageSelectionError || item.googleImageCredentialError || (item.autoPublish ? 'Quality gate did not pass.' : 'AUTO_PUBLISH_ENABLED=false. Dry run only.'),
    sourceMode: item.sourceMode,
    title: item.title,
    slug: item.slug,
    sourceUrls: item.sourceUrls || [],
    approved: item.approved,
    autoPublish: item.autoPublish,
    coverImage: item.coverImage || null,
    coverImageReady: item.coverImageReady === true,
    coverImageSource: item.coverImageSource || '',
    imageSearchQuery: item.imageSearchQuery || '',
    imageSelectionError: item.imageSelectionError || '',
    imageProxyFailureReason: item.imageProxyFailureReason || '',
    googleImageSearchStatusCode: item.googleImageSearchStatusCode || null,
    marbleKeyPrefix: item.marbleKeyPrefix,
    quality: item.quality,
    checkedAt: new Date().toISOString()
  }
}];

nodes.name: Needs Recovery Retry?
nodes.notes: If quality gate fails on the first draft, generate one recovery/repair draft before skipping.

nodes.name: Build Recovery Prompt
nodes.notes: Creates a stricter regeneration prompt when the first article is rejected by quality gate.

nodes.name: Gemini Generate Recovery Article
nodes.notes: Recovery article generation. Retries transient Gemini capacity/API errors.

nodes.name: Gemini Quality Gate Recovery
nodes.notes: Quality gate for the recovery draft. Retries transient Gemini capacity/API errors.

nodes.name: Prepare Marble Payload Recovery
nodes.notes: Builds an image-safe draft Marble payload. The v8.1 Google image stage later replaces or adds coverImage only after the same-origin proxy has been verified.

nodes.name: Check Existing Marble Post
nodes.notes: Preflight slug lookup. A 200 becomes PATCH, a 404 becomes POST. Other statuses are rejected with a clear error.

nodes.name: Prepare Marble Upsert
nodes.notes: Builds an idempotent create-or-update request from the slug preflight result.

parameters.jsCode:
const item = $input.first().json;
return [{ json: { ...item, writeAttempt: Number(item.writeAttempt || 0) + 1 } }];

nodes.name: Evaluate Marble Write
nodes.notes: Recovers from duplicate-slug 409s, PATCH/POST races, throttling, and transient Marble 5xx responses.

nodes.name: Wait Before Marble Retry
nodes.notes: Backoff before retrying transient Marble write errors.

nodes.name: Verify Marble Published Post
nodes.notes: Read-after-write verification. Retries until Marble exposes the slug with status=published, preventing a premature website revalidation that can cache a 404.

nodes.name: Prepare Post-Publish Verification
nodes.notes: Uses the canonical slug returned by Marble, builds the public URL, and appends the detail and index paths to the optional revalidation endpoint.

nodes.name: Verify Public Blog URL
nodes.notes: Retries the canonical /blog/[slug] page for up to roughly 80 seconds. A persistent 404/5xx fails the run instead of reporting a false publish success.

nodes.name: Assert Public Blog Page
nodes.notes: Rejects soft-404 and generic server-error pages even if the host responds with HTTP 200.

parameters.content:
## v8.1 Google images + 404 safety

**Required publishing:** `MARBLE_API_KEY` (private `msk_...` with `posts_read` + `posts_write`), `MARBLE_CATEGORY_ID`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL`.

**Required Google image search:** `GOOGLE_CSE_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_CSE_CX`. Create a Google Programmable Search Engine with image search and whole-web search enabled.

**Image controls:** `GOOGLE_IMAGE_SEARCH_ENABLED=true`, `REQUIRE_COVER_IMAGE=true`, `REQUIRE_GOOGLE_IMAGE=false`, `GOOGLE_IMAGE_SOURCE_SITES`, `GOOGLE_IMAGE_ALLOWED_HOSTS`, `GOOGLE_IMAGE_RIGHTS` (optional CC filter), `GOOGLE_IMAGE_MIN_WIDTH`, `GOOGLE_IMAGE_MIN_HEIGHT`, `GOOGLE_IMAGE_MAX_BYTES`, `BLOG_IMAGE_PROXY_PATH=/api/blog/image`, `APPEND_COVER_IMAGE_CREDIT=true`.

**Fallback:** the workflow uses `GOOGLE_IMAGE_FALLBACK_URL`, `MARBLE_DEFAULT_COVER_IMAGE_URL`, `BLOG_DEFAULT_COVER_IMAGE_URL`, then `${NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL}/images/blog/default-cover.svg`.

**Website requirement:** implement GET + HEAD `/api/blog/image` as described in the supplied Google Antigravity prompt. It must proxy only approved remote image hosts and return an image fallback rather than a 404.

**Optional revalidation:** `HUYGEN_REVALIDATE_URL`, `HUYGEN_REVALIDATE_SECRET`.

**Retry controls:** `MARBLE_WRITE_MAX_ATTEMPTS` (default 4).

nodes.name: Prepare Google Image Search
nodes.notes: Builds a relevant, licensed-source Google image query from the approved article title, cluster, and keyword. No API key is written into workflow data.

nodes.name: Has Google Image Search Credentials?
nodes.notes: False branch uses a safe fallback. Set GOOGLE_CSE_API_KEY and GOOGLE_CSE_CX to enable Google image discovery.

parameters.jsonQuery: ={{ { key: $env.GOOGLE_CSE_API_KEY || $env.GOOGLE_CUSTOM_SEARCH_API_KEY, cx: $env.GOOGLE_CSE_CX || $env.GOOGLE_PROGRAMMABLE_SEARCH_ENGINE_ID, q: $json.imageSearchQuery, searchType: 'image', safe: 'active', num: $json.googleImageResultCount, imgSize: $json.googleImageSize, imgType: 'photo', gl: $json.googleImageCountry, lr: $json.googleImageLanguage, ...($json.googleImageRights ? { rights: $json.googleImageRights } : {}) } }}

nodes.name: Google Custom Image Search
nodes.notes: Official Google Programmable Search JSON API image search. SafeSearch is active. Search is restricted by default to configured stock/media source sites; GOOGLE_IMAGE_RIGHTS can optionally add Google licensing filters.

nodes.name: Select Relevant Google Image
nodes.notes: Scores Google results by semantic overlap, image dimensions, 16:9 suitability, file size, format, and trusted image host. Defaults to Unsplash, Pexels, Pixabay, and Wikimedia delivery hosts.

nodes.name: Has Relevant Google Image?
nodes.notes: Only eligible, landscape-oriented, trusted-host images proceed to the same-origin proxy check.

nodes.name: Verify Same-Origin Image Proxy
nodes.notes: Checks the website image proxy before Marble receives the cover URL. The Antigravity prompt creates this HEAD/GET route with host, MIME, redirect, size, SSRF, cache, and fallback controls.

nodes.name: Use Safe Cover Fallback
nodes.notes: Preserves publishing context when Google credentials are absent or no candidate passes the filters.

nodes.name: Finalize Verified Cover Image
nodes.notes: Only puts the verified same-origin proxy URL into Marble. If verification fails, it falls back to a configured URL or /images/blog/default-cover.svg; coverImage:null is never sent.

nodes.name: Image Ready to Publish?
nodes.notes: If REQUIRE_COVER_IMAGE=true and no verified/fallback image exists, the workflow returns a clear skipped result instead of publishing a broken post.

meta.enhancedBy: ChatGPT workflow edit v8.1: Google Programmable Search image discovery, licensed-source filtering, same-origin image proxy verification, cover-image fallback, idempotent Marble upsert, revalidation, and public 404/server-error verification
